title: B3. Ambiguity Detection (Pronoun Disambiguation)
label: mx1_transmute_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B3-ambiguity-detection-pronoun-disambiguation
Title: B3. Ambiguity Detection (Pronoun Disambiguation)

Problem:
"The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic."

Which option correctly explains the pronoun antecedent?

Answer choices: 5 options.

Grading target:
Exact match on the single letter.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
The task requires analyzing a specific linguistic ambiguity involving pronoun antecedents to determine the correct explanation among five options.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Analyze the linguistic ambiguity in the following text: 'The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic.' The core task is to identify the corre
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: The investigator
JUSTIFICATION: The investigator is the subject performing the actions.

grading.grading_notes: The submitted answer identifies 'The investigator' as the antecedent, whereas the authoritative reference states the sentence is 'Ambiguous' because the pronouns 'he' and 'his' could grammatically refer to either the investigator or the witness. The grader type hint requires an exact choice letter match.
